text: Several studies have shown that THs regulate BDNF expression in the brain (Koibuchi et al., 1999; Koibuchi and Chin, 2000; Sui and Li, 2010), with the subsequent neurodevelopmental consequences, as described in the direct KER. For example, during the early cortical network development TH has been shown to regulate the morphology and function of the GABAergic neurons (Westerholz et al., 2010) and BDNF is one of the mediators of this regulation (Binder and Scharfman, 2004; Gilbert and Lasley, 2013).  In view of the above evidence, it has been suggested that the thyroid insufficiency triggered by inhibition of TPO or NIS functions, resulting in decreased TH synthesis and subsequent lowered TH levels in serum and brain, may lead to reduction of the levels of BDNF mRNA or protein in the developmental brain.
